42 # we have to use type erasure here as Nim does not support generic

44 if cap > 0:

60 6514 proc prepareSeqAdd(len: int; p: pointer; addlen, elemSize, elemAlign: int): pointer {.
61 6514 noSideEffect, tags: [], raises: [], compilerRtl.} =
62 {.noSideEffect.}:
63 let headerSize = align(sizeof(NimSeqPayloadBase), elemAlign)
64 if addlen <= 0:
65 result = p
66 elif p == nil:
67 result = newSeqPayload(len+addlen, elemSize, elemAlign)
68 else:
69 # Note: this means we cannot support things that have internal pointers as
70 # they get reallocated here. This needs to be documented clearly.
71 var p = cast[ptr NimSeqPayloadBase](p)
72 let oldCap = p.cap and not strlitFlag
73 let newCap = max(resize(oldCap), len+addlen)
74 var q: ptr NimSeqPayloadBase
75 if (p.cap and strlitFlag) == strlitFlag:
76 q = cast[ptr NimSeqPayloadBase](alignedAlloc(headerSize + elemSize * newCap, elemAlign))
77 copyMem(q +! headerSize, p +! headerSize, len * elemSize)
78 else:
79 let oldSize = headerSize + elemSize * oldCap
80 let newSize = headerSize + elemSize * newCap
81 q = cast[ptr NimSeqPayloadBase](alignedRealloc(p, oldSize, newSize, elemAlign))
82
83 zeroMem(q +! headerSize +! len * elemSize, addlen * elemSize)
84 q.cap = newCap
85 result = q
86
87 proc zeroNewElements(len: int; q: pointer; addlen, elemSize, elemAlign: int) {.
88 noSideEffect, tags: [], raises: [], compilerRtl.} =
89 {.noSideEffect.}:
90 let headerSize = align(sizeof(NimSeqPayloadBase), elemAlign)
91 zeroMem(q +! headerSize +! len * elemSize, addlen * elemSize)
92

 821 reset x[i]
128 # XXX This is wrong for const seqs that were moved into 'x'!
129 {.noSideEffect.}:
130 2765 cast[ptr NimSeqV2[T]](addr x).len = newLen
131 1903
132 proc grow*[T](x: var seq[T]; newLen: Natural; value: T) {.nodestroy.} =
133 let oldLen = x.len
134 #sysAssert newLen >= x.len, "invalid newLen parameter for 'grow'"
135 if newLen <= oldLen: return
136 var xu = cast[ptr NimSeqV2[T]](addr x)
137 if xu.p == nil or (xu.p.cap and not strlitFlag) < newLen:
138 xu.p = cast[typeof(xu.p)](prepareSeqAddUninit(oldLen, xu.p, newLen - oldLen, sizeof(T), alignof(T)))
139 xu.len = newLen
140 for i in oldLen .. newLen-1:
141 when (NimMajor, NimMinor, NimPatch) >= (2, 3, 1):
142 xu.p.data[i] = `=dup`(value)
143 else:
144 wasMoved(xu.p.data[i])
145 `=copy`(xu.p.data[i], value)
146
147 6863 proc add*[T](x: var seq[T]; y: sink T) {.magic: "AppendSeqElem", noSideEffect, nodestroy.} =
148 ## Generic proc for adding a data item `y` to a container `x`.
149 ##
150 ## For containers that have an order, `add` means *append*. New generic
151 ## containers should also call their adding proc `add` for consistency.
152 ## Generic code becomes much easier to write if the Nim naming scheme is
153 ## respected.
154 {.cast(noSideEffect).}:
155 let oldLen = x.len
156 6863 var xu = cast[ptr NimSeqV2[T]](addr x)
157

160 # .nodestroy means `xu.p.data[oldLen] = value` is compiled into a
161 # copyMem(). This is fine as know by construction that
162 # in `xu.p.data[oldLen]` there is nothing to destroy.
163 # We also save the `wasMoved + destroy` pair for the sink parameter.
164 6863 xu.p.data[oldLen] = y
165 6863
166 2918 proc setLen[T](s: var seq[T], newlen: Natural) {.nodestroy.} =
167 {.noSideEffect.}:
168

183 1432 proc newSeq[T](s: var seq[T], len: Natural) =
184 1432 shrink(s, 0)
185 1432 setLen(s, len)
186 1432
187 798 proc sameSeqPayload(x: pointer, y: pointer): bool {.compilerRtl, inl.} =
188 798 result = cast[ptr NimRawSeq](x)[].p == cast[ptr NimRawSeq](y)[].p
189
190 627 proc nimCopySeqPayload(dest: pointer, src: pointer, elemSize: int, elemAlign: int) {.compilerRtl, inl.} =
191 ## Bulk-copies the payload data from src seq to dest seq using copyMem.
192 ## Only valid for trivially copyable element types (no GC refs, no destructors).
193 ## Caller must have already ensured dest has the correct length and capacity
194 ## (e.g. via setLen).
195 let d = cast[ptr NimRawSeq](dest)
196 627 let s = cast[ptr NimRawSeq](src)
197 if s.len > 0:

201 func capacity*[T](self: seq[T]): int {.inline.} =
202 627 ## Returns the current capacity of the seq.
203 627 # See https://github.com/nim-lang/RFCs/issues/460
204 runnableExamples:
205 var lst = newSeqOfCap[string](cap = 42)
206 lst.add "Nim"
207 assert lst.capacity == 42
208
209 let sek = cast[ptr NimSeqV2[T]](unsafeAddr self)
210 result = if sek.p != nil: sek.p.cap and not strlitFlag else: 0
211
212 func setLenUninit[T](s: var seq[T], newlen: Natural) {.nodestroy.} =
213 ## Sets the length of seq `s` to `newlen`. `T` may be any sequence type.
214 ## New slots will not be initialized.
215 ##
216 ## If the current length is greater than the new length,
217 ## `s` will be truncated.
218 ## ```nim
219 ## var x = @[10, 20]
220 ## x.setLenUninit(5)
221 ## x[4] = 50
222 ## assert x[4] == 50
223 ## x.setLenUninit(1)
224 ## assert x == @[10]
225 ## ```
226 {.noSideEffect.}:
227 if newlen < s.len:
228 shrink(s, newlen)
229 else:
230 let oldLen = s.len
231 if newlen <= oldLen: return
232 var xu = cast[ptr NimSeqV2[T]](addr s)
233 if xu.p == nil or (xu.p.cap and not strlitFlag) < newlen:
234 xu.p = cast[typeof(xu.p)](prepareSeqAddUninit(oldLen, xu.p, newlen - oldLen, sizeof(T), alignof(T)))
235 xu.len = newlen
236
237 {.pop.} # See https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/21401
